evals.prompt: Download my EOM Import Variogram so I can use it locally.
evals.expected_output: Import succeeds and summary confirms the variogram properties including sill, nugget, and structure count.

evals.prompt: Import my EOM Import Point Set so I can check it has the GRADE and DOMAIN attributes.
evals.expected_output: Import succeeds and summary confirms the point set with its attributes (GRADE, DOMAIN), point count, and coordinate reference system.

evals.prompt: Import my EOM Import Block Model so I can inspect its grid dimensions.
evals.expected_output: Import succeeds and summary confirms the block model grid dimensions and block size.

evals.prompt: Publish my local variogram EOM Stage Variogram New to the workspace as a brand new object at object_path /evals/staging-workflow/eom-stage-variogram-new.
evals.expected_output: Publish succeeds for the staged-only variogram using mode=create and the provided object_path. A new workspace object is created with a valid object ID and version ID.

evals.prompt: Publish my local point set EOM Stage Point Set New to the workspace as a new object at object_path /evals/staging-workflow/eom-stage-point-set-new.
evals.expected_output: Publish succeeds for the staged-only point set using mode=create and the provided object_path. A new workspace object is created with a valid object ID and version ID.

evals.prompt: Publish my local block model EOM Stage Block Model New to the workspace as a new object at object_path /evals/staging-workflow/eom-stage-block-model-new.
evals.expected_output: Publish succeeds for the staged-only regular block model using mode=create and the provided object_path. A new workspace object is created with a valid object ID and version ID.

evals.prompt: Use my already staged EOM Both Variogram and publish it back as an updated version of the same Evo object.
evals.expected_output: Publish as new version succeeds for EOM Both Variogram without requiring a re-import. The object ID stays the same but the version ID changes.

evals.prompt: Use my already staged EOM Both Point Set and publish it back as an updated version.
evals.expected_output: Publish as new version succeeds for EOM Both Point Set without requiring a re-import. The object ID stays the same but the version ID changes.

evals.prompt: What objects do I currently have locally?
evals.expected_output: A current staged inventory is returned in plain language using available staging fields (at minimum object type plus stage identity/source reference details for each staged entry).

evals.prompt: Import EOM Import Variogram Discard from the workspace, then delete the local copy.
evals.expected_output: Import succeeds. After discarding, EOM Import Variogram Discard is no longer present in the session.

evals.prompt: Import EOM Import Point Set Duplicate from the workspace, then try importing EOM Import Point Set Duplicate again without discarding it first.
evals.expected_output: The second import raises a duplicate-name error that states an object named EOM Import Point Set Duplicate is already staged and must be discarded first.

evals.prompt: Show me which object types are available for local staging and whether each one supports import and publish.
evals.expected_output: The response lists stageable object types and includes support details for import and publish modes.

evals.prompt: Create a local search neighborhood named EOM Eval Search Neighborhood with max_samples 24, min_samples 8, major 180, semi_major 120, minor 80, dip_azimuth 0, dip 0, and pitch 0.
evals.expected_output: Creation succeeds for a new local search neighborhood named EOM Eval Search Neighborhood with the requested configuration.

evals.prompt: List the available interactions for variogram objects.
evals.expected_output: The response includes variogram interaction names and descriptions, including get_summary.

evals.prompt: Run the get_summary interaction on my staged variogram named EOM Stage Variogram Interaction.
evals.expected_output: Interaction invocation succeeds and returns a variogram summary for EOM Stage Variogram Interaction.
